reasoning: The informal statement, in the Munkres sense, asserts two things: (1) T satisfies the basis axioms, and (2) the topology generated by T is different from the lower limit topology. The Lean `IsTopologicalBasis T` in Mathlib is defined with respect to the ambient topology and has three conditions, the last being `eq_generateFrom : ‹TopologicalSpace α› = generateFrom s`. Since ℝ carries its default (standard Euclidean) topology, the Lean statement additionally asserts that the standard topology on ℝ equals `generateFrom T` — an extra claim beyond just "T is a basis". Thus the Lean statement logically implies the informal one (stripping the extra claim leaves the basis axioms) but adds a conclusion (T generates the Euclidean topology on ℝ) not present in the informal text, making it strictly stronger than what was asked.
